description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a boxy, angular design and a prominent, thick bezel framing the display. The front features a square screen displaying a landscape image, surrounded by a light gray plastic bezel with a slightly protruding lower edge. The monitor is supported by a wide, rounded pedestal base that provides stability. The rear of the monitor extends outward in a trapezoidal shape, typical of older cathode ray tube displays, and is constructed from the same light gray plastic as the bezel. No visible buttons, ports, or branding are present on the exterior surfaces. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a matte finish and minimal decorative elements.
description: A boxy, light gray CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel and a rounded pedestal base, featuring a square display and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the square display with a thick bezel, a wide pedestal base, and no visible buttons or logos.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the deep, trapezoidal rear housing and the rounded pedestal base, with no visible controls or ports.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the full depth of the monitor's housing and the flat back panel, with no visible features or branding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the deep rear housing and pedestal base, with the front bezel edge just visible.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and made of matte light gray plastic, with squared corners and no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, rounded pedestal base made of the same light gray plastic, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
